steps: SETTING UP THE GENIE II
Take a swab
Preparing a sample batch (up to 8 samples different testees)
Plug In Genie® II (Heat Block and Reader, Genie, GEN2-01)
Set the block temperature to 65 °C . Make sure temperatures are reached to set temperature before placing the assay tubes into heat-block for amplification.
Open sample swab with caution and transfer swab to a blue tube, which contains elution solution and mix/swirl the swab 4-5 times.
Pipette 5 µL of eluted swab sample from the blue tube into a tube on the red strip, which contains SARS-CoV-2 master mix. Pipette up and down 2 times to mix assay components, then close the lid

Pipette 5 µL of eluted swab sample from blue tube into a tube on the green strip, which contains the internal control master mix. NOTE: samples from each patient should be put in the same tube number on the red strip and green strip
Hand testee a sterile swab, packaged in 15 mL falcon tube, and instruct patient on use
Testee to remove swab from 15 mL falcon tube
Testee returns swab to 15 mL falcon tube
Testee inserts swab into each nasal cavity, rotating for 10 seconds
Testee hands tube to supervisor
Discard the swab in a biohazardous waste container
Repeat steps 4.1 through 4.4 for all samples in the batch
Running a sample
On the Genie II touch screen, select blocks A and B
Run program called "FireLAMP"
Wait for incubation to complete (~30 minutes for negative samples)
Preparing for Next Block
Discard all used blue tubes, red strips, and green strips in biohazard container
Wait 5 minutes for Genie II to cool before starting analyis of a new batch.
Setting up Workplace with Reagents
Take Red Strips and Geen Strips out of freezer and thaw for 1-2 min at room temperature
Set up biohazard disposal
Set Blue Tubes in work area
Open the Genie® II (Heat Block and Reader, Genie, GEN2-01) . Place the red batch into Block A and the green batch into Block B. Close the Genie II.
Interpretation of results
Touch results tab.

Record results based on the "results column" column for block A and block B. Positive result in the block with the red strip (typically block A) indicate a positive result for COVID19 . Positive results in the block with the green strip (typically block B) indicate that the sample was taken correctly.
Record results
creator: Daniel Benner
